schema.name: TZPID source truth generated from canonical equation master

theory.canonical_statement:
## 6) Equations & criteria (concise toolkit) - **Buoyancy acceleration (ideal gas / Boussinesq approx):** \[ a_b \approx g\, eta\,\Delta T, \qquad  eta\approx rac{1}{T}. \] - **Rayleigh number (for onset of convection in layers):** \[ \mathrm{Ra}= rac{g eta\Delta T\,L^3}{
u\kappa}. \] - **Schwarzschild criterion (stellar convection):** \[ 
abla_{m rad} =  rac{3\kappa P L_r}{16\pi a c G M_r T^4} \quad	ext{; convection if }
abla_{m rad}>
abla_{m ad}. \] (Where \(\kappa\) is opacity, \(L_r\) local luminosity, \(M_r\) mass interior, etc.) - **MHD momentum (ideal MHD):** \[ ho\left( rac{\partial \mathbf{v}}{\partial t} + \mathbf{v}\cdot
abla\mathbf{v}ight) = -
abla p + \mathbf{J}	imes\mathbf{B} + ho\mathbf{g} + 	ext{viscous terms}. \] Couple this with induction and energy equations for a full picture.
theory.technical_interpretation: Equations & Criteria (Concise Toolkit): Ra is an algebraic definition, written Ra=(gβΔ T L^3)/(νκ).. It is an algebraic definition expressing the quantity directly in terms of the variables on its right-hand side. It is built from β, Δ, ν, κ, g, T, defining Ra. Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.

formation.inputs: β, Δ, ν, κ, g, T
formation.note: Introduced in the derivation where rayleigh number (for onset of convection in layers)
